properties.title: Linenhill Barracks
properties.text: As the capital of a subjugated nation with a long history of rebellion, Dublin in 1904 was quite literally ringed with military barracks

properties.title: Sweny's Pharmacy
properties.text: In 1904, James Joyce consulted with Sweny for his novel Ulysses. Here the fictional character Mr Bloom picks up his lucky charm – a lemon soap.

properties.title: Abby Theater
properties.text: Also known as the National Theater of Ireland this institution was founded in 1904 by W.B. Yeats and Lady Gregory “to bring upon the stage the deeper emotions of Ireland”. In the first chapter, Telemachus Milligan refers to the so-called Irish Literary Revival.

properties.title: Mortello Tower Sandycove
properties.text: The novel's first action, 'Buck Mulligan came from the stairhead,' situates the reader on the roof of a small military tower in Sandycove, seven miles from Dublin on the southeastern shores of Dublin Bay.
